allowedDifferences.reason: The exact foreclosed-unwrap self-destination guard changes Wrapper compiler output. Security 04 records complete Security-03-relative Wrapper opcode diffs, raw and normalized hashes and sizes, EIP-170 checks, exact source placement, and behavior provenance; standalone PartialCommonOwnership remains exact.

The exact foreclosed-unwrap self-destination guard changes Wrapper compiler output. Security 04 records complete Security-03-relative Wrapper opcode diffs, raw and normalized hashes and sizes, EIP-170 checks, exact source placement, and behavior provenance; standalone PartialCommonOwnership remains exact.

The exact foreclosed-unwrap self-destination guard changes Wrapper compiler output. Security 04 records complete Security-03-relative Wrapper opcode diffs, raw and normalized hashes and sizes, EIP-170 checks, exact source placement, and behavior provenance; standalone PartialCommonOwnership remains exact.

guard.adjacentPath: The same guard preserves metadata after a raw non-safe wrapped-token transfer to Wrapper without reclassifying that transfer's economics.

callerOutcomes.nonOriginator: Error(string): Wrap originator only, including after materialized foreclosure
callerOutcomes.originator: DestinationContractAddress() when the wrapped owner is Wrapper

error.declarationChange: none; reuse the inherited Remittance custom error

guard.recovery: A takeover from contract custody restores an external wrapped owner, after which the original operator can unwrap to that owner.
guard.rollback: The guard emits no committed logs and preserves wrapped ownership, metadata, underlying custody, balances, approvals, tax, deposit, remittance, and lock state.

behaviorEvidence.pendingForeclosure: Owner capture occurs before _burn tax collection, so a not-yet-materialized foreclosure retains its successful nine-event collection, foreclosure, burn, and underlying delivery behavior.
